1	\chapter{The Quot scheme}
2	\label{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GradedHilbertSerre.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE. This chapter packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
6	% Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
7	% \(\mathrm{Quot}^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\): the per-fiber Hilbert polynomial, the
8	% Quot functor of \(T\)-flat coherent quotients of \(E_T\) on
9	% \(X_T = X \times_S T\), and the Grassmannian \emph{scheme} together with
10	% its representability. The Quot functor underlies the relative Picard
11	% functor via the Hilbert scheme \(\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}\)
12	% -- the case \(E = \mathcal{O}_X\) of the Quot functor.
13	%
14	% LOAD-BEARING SUB-PREREQUISITE. The Quot construction embeds Quot as a
15	% locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian
16	% \(\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))\).
17	% Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} -- only
18	% a linear-algebra Grassmannian as a finite-rank-quotient variety. The
19	% scheme-level Grassmannian over an arbitrary noetherian base is therefore an
20	% in-project sub-build. This chapter introduces it as
21	% \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} and states its representability
22	% (\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).
23	
24	\section{Setup and motivation}
25	\label{sec:quot_setup}
26	
27	Throughout this chapter, let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a
28	projective morphism, \(L\) a relatively very ample line bundle on \(X\) over \(S\),
29	and \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\). The Quot construction packages the
30	\(T\)-flat coherent quotients \(E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of the
31	pull-back \(E_T\) to \(X_T = X \times_S T\), modulo the equivalence
32	\(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) defined by
33	\(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). Fixing the Hilbert polynomial
34	\(\Phi(\lambda) = \chi(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}(\lambda)) \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
35	(constant on connected \(T\) by flatness)
36	stratifies the Quot functor into pieces \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\), each of
37	which Grothendieck proved is representable by a projective \(S\)-scheme.
38	This chapter develops the foundations of that picture: the Hilbert
39	polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}), the Quot functor
40	(\cref{def:quot_functor}), and the Grassmannian scheme
41	(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) with its representability
42	(\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).
43	
44	% SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1;
45	% FGA Explained Ch.~5, AMS MSM 123 (read from
46	% references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L287-L500).
47	% The Hartshorne reference for the polynomial-eventually
48	% property of Euler characteristics is [Hartshorne], III.5.2 (the scanned
49	% PDF at references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf has no text layer, so
50	% the verbatim source-quote is taken from
51	% Nitsure \S 1; Hartshorne III.5.2 is cited in prose as the textbook
52	% reference).
53	
54	\section{Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf}
55	\label{sec:hilbert_polynomial}
56	
57	\begin{definition}\leanok
58	[Hilbert polynomial]
59	  \label{def:hilbert_polynomial}
60	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial}
61	  \uses{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}
62	  % ENCODING. `def:hilbert_polynomial` is formalized via the GRADED HILBERT
63	  % FUNCTION of the section module ⊕_{m≥0} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})
64	  % (def:sectionGradedModule), whose polynomiality is extracted in
65	  % thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule from the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality
66	  % lemma (lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational) together with the Mathlib lemma
67	  % Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly (lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib). The
68	  % construction uses only H^0; for m≫0 it agrees with the Euler characteristic
69	  % χ by Serre vanishing, so it is the classical Hilbert polynomial and no
70	  % numerical invariant is lost.
71	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
72	  % Polynomials" (read from
73	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L478).
74	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
75	  % "Let \(X\) be a finite type scheme over
76	  %  a field \(k\), together with a line bundle \(L\).
77	  %  Recall that if \(F\) is a coherent sheaf on
78	  %  \(X\) whose support is proper over \(k\), then
79	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\) of \(F\) is defined by
80	  %  the function
81	  %  \(\){\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
82	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})\(\)
83	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
84	  %  coherence and properness conditions. The fact that \(\chi(F(m))\)
85	  %  is indeed a polynomial in \(m\) under the above assumption is a special
86	  %  case of what is known as Snapper's Lemma (see Kleiman [K] for a proof).
87	  %
88	  %  Let \(X\to S\) be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
89	  %  and let \(L\) be a line bundle on \(X\). Let \(\F\)
90	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
91	  %  \(X\) whose schematic support is proper over \(S\). Then for each \(s\in S\),
92	  %  we get a polynomial \({\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]\)
93	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
94	  %  the restriction \(\F_s = F|_{X_s}\) of \(\F\) to the fiber \(X_s\) over \(s\),
95	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle \(L_s = L|_{X_s}\).
96	  %  If \(\F\) is flat over \(S\) then the function \(s\mapsto {\Phi}_s\)
97	  %  from the set of points of \(S\) to the polynomial ring \(\Q[\lambda]\) is
98	  %  known to be locally constant on \(S\)."
99	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
100	  III.5.2.}
101	  Let \(X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism of noetherian schemes and let \(L\)
102	  be a line bundle on \(X\). For a coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) whose
103	  schematic support is proper over \(S\) and a point \(s \in S\), write \(X_s\)
104	  for the fibre over \(s\), \(\mathcal{F}_s = \mathcal{F}|_{X_s}\) and
105	  \(L_s = L|_{X_s}\). The \emph{Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}\) at \(s\)
106	  relative to \(L\)} is the unique polynomial
107	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\) that agrees, for all
108	  \(m \gg 0\), with the graded Hilbert function
109	  \[
110	    m \;\longmapsto\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr)
111	  \]
112	  of the section module
113	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
114	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}), viewed as a finitely generated graded module
115	  over the homogeneous section ring
116	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
117	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}). Existence and uniqueness of
118	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) are the content of
119	  \cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}.
120	
121	  This is the classical Hilbert polynomial. When \(\mathcal{F}_s\) is coherent
122	  and \(L_s\) is ample on the proper \(\kappa(s)\)-scheme \(X_s\), Serre vanishing
123	  gives \(H^i(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}) = 0\) for all \(i > 0\)
124	  and \(m \gg 0\), so for \(m \gg 0\)
125	  \[
126	    \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
127	    \;=\; \chi(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
128	    \;=\; \sum_{i \ge 0} (-1)^i \dim_{\kappa(s)} H^i(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}),
129	  \]
130	  and hence \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) coincides with the Euler-characteristic
131	  Hilbert polynomial of Snapper's Lemma -- no numerical invariant is lost. The
132	  construction above, however, uses only the global sections \(H^0\): the higher
133	  cohomology enters solely through the classical statement that the two functions
134	  agree for \(m \gg 0\), never through the definition of \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\)
135	  itself. When \(\mathcal{F}\) is \(S\)-flat the function
136	  \(s \mapsto \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is locally constant on \(S\), so \(\Phi\) is
137	  well-defined on connected components.
138	\end{definition}
139	
140	\section{Graded Hilbert polynomial}
141	\label{sec:graded_hilbert_polynomial}
142	
143	This section gives the graded-Hilbert-function construction that underlies
144	\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Working over a single fibre \(X_s\) with its
145	ample line bundle \(L_s\) and coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}_s\), it builds the
146	section graded ring (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) and module
147	(\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}), records their finite generation
148	(\cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}), and extracts the Hilbert polynomial
149	(\cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}) by combining the graded
150	Hilbert--Serre rationality of the Hilbert series
151	(\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}) with Mathlib's polynomial-extraction
152	lemma (\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}). The route uses only \(H^0\)
153	(global sections), so it is independent of higher coherent cohomology.
154	
155	\begin{definition}
156	[Section graded ring]
157	  \label{def:sectionGradedRing}
158	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedRing}
159	  % NOTE: Formalization is blocked on the absence of tensor products for sheaves of modules in
160	  % Mathlib at the pinned commit. The degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
161	  % requires a tensor power \(L_s^{\otimes m}\) of a line bundle; this infrastructure (tensor
162	  % product of sheaves of modules with lax-monoidal global sections) is not yet available, making
163	  % this definition a prerequisite sub-build.
164	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
165	  % sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert Polynomials" (read from
166	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
167	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
168	  % "Let $X$ be a finite type scheme over
169	  %  a field $k$, together with a line bundle $L$.
170	  %  Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
171	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
172	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
173	  %  the function
174	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
175	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
176	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
177	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
178	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); the section graded
179	  ring is the homogeneous coordinate ring of [Hartshorne], I.7.}
180	  Let \(s \in S\) and let \(L_s\) be a line bundle on the fibre \(X_s\), a scheme
181	  of finite type over the residue field \(\kappa(s)\). The \emph{section graded
182	  ring} of \(L_s\) is the graded commutative ring
183	  \[
184	    R(X_s, L_s) \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
185	  \]
186	  with degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\) and multiplication
187	  the tensor product of sections
188	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
189	  \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m'}) \to \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\),
190	  \(\sigma \otimes \tau \mapsto \sigma \cdot \tau\). Its degree-zero component is
191	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{O}_{X_s})\), which contains \(\kappa(s)\). When \(X_s\) is
192	  proper over \(\kappa(s)\) and \(L_s\) is ample, \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely
193	  generated -- hence Noetherian -- graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra (a suitable
194	  Veronese power of \(L_s\) is very ample and embeds \(X_s\) into a projective
195	  space, exhibiting \(R(X_s, L_s)\) up to a finite extension as a quotient of a
196	  polynomial ring).
197	\end{definition}
198	
199	\begin{definition}
200	[Section graded module]
201	  \label{def:sectionGradedModule}
202	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule}
203	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing}
204	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
205	  % Polynomials" (read from
206	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
207	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
208	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
209	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
210	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
211	  %  the function
212	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
213	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
214	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
215	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
216	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
217	  I.7.}
218	  Let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on the fibre \(X_s\) and \(L_s\) a
219	  line bundle on \(X_s\). The \emph{section graded module} of \(\mathcal{F}_s\)
220	  twisted by \(L_s\) is the graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module
221	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing})
222	  \[
223	    M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)
224	    \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
225	  \]
226	  with degree-\(m\) component the \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space
227	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) and scalar action given
228	  by the section tensor product
229	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
230	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m'})
231	  \to \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\). Its graded Hilbert
232	  function is \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)}
233	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\).
234	\end{definition}
235	
236	\begin{lemma}
237	[Finite generation of the section graded module]
238	  \label{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}
239	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule_fg}
240	  \uses{def:sectionGradedModule, def:sectionGradedRing}
241	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
242	  % Polynomials" (read from
243	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
244	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
245	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
246	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
247	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
248	  %  the function
249	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
250	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
251	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
252	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
253	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); Serre's theorem on
254	  ample line bundles, [Hartshorne], I.7 / II.5.}
255	  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
256	  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
257	  Then the section graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) is a
258	  Noetherian graded ring, and the section graded module
259	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}) is a finitely
260	  generated graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module. In particular each component
261	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a finite-dimensional
262	  \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space.
263	\end{lemma}
264	
265	\begin{proof}
266	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, def:sectionGradedModule}
267	  Since \(L_s\) is ample and \(X_s\) is proper over \(\kappa(s)\), a Veronese
268	  power \(L_s^{\otimes e}\) is very ample and gives a closed immersion
269	  \(X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\). By Serre's theorem the section
270	  graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded
271	  \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra, so it is Noetherian. For the coherent sheaf
272	  \(\mathcal{F}_s\), the same ampleness yields, again by Serre, that
273	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a
274	  finitely generated graded module over \(R(X_s, L_s)\): the twists
275	  \(\mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\) are globally generated for
276	  \(m \gg 0\), and the finitely many generating sections in low degrees together
277	  with the multiplication maps generate \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) over
278	  \(R(X_s, L_s)\). Finite dimensionality of each component over \(\kappa(s)\)
279	  follows from coherence and properness (finiteness of cohomology of coherent
280	  sheaves on a proper scheme over a field).
281	\end{proof}
282	
283	\begin{lemma}[Existence and uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial of a rational
284	  graded series]
285	  \label{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
286	  \lean{Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly}
287	  \mathlibok
288	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
289	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.HilbertPoly}).}
290	  Let \(F\) be a field of characteristic zero, let \(p \in F[X]\), and let
291	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then there is a unique polynomial \(h \in F[X]\) such that
292	  for some \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) and all \(n > N\) the coefficient of \(X^n\) in the
293	  power-series expansion of \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d} \in F\llbracket X\rrbracket\)
294	  equals \(h(n)\):
295	  \[
296	    \exists!\, h \in F[X],\ \exists N,\ \forall n > N : \;
297	    \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1-X)^{-d} \bigr) \;=\; h(n).
298	  \]
299	  Equivalently, the eventual coefficient function of any rational power series
300	  with denominator a power of \(1 - X\) is, for \(n \gg 0\), given by a unique
301	  polynomial \(h\) (Mathlib's \(\mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\)). The hypothesis
302	  \([\mathrm{CharZero}\,F]\) is satisfied by \(F = \mathbb{Q}\), which is the
303	  instance used to obtain \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\).
304	\end{lemma}
305	
306	\begin{lemma}[Additivity of \(\dim_\kappa\) on a short exact sequence
307	  (rank--nullity)]
308	  \label{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}
309	  \lean{Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank}
310	  \mathlibok
311	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
312	  (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.RankNullity}).}
313	  Let \(\kappa\) be a field, \(V\) a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space and
314	  \(W \subseteq V\) a subspace. Then
315	  \[
316	    \dim_\kappa (V / W) + \dim_\kappa W \;=\; \dim_\kappa V .
317	  \]
318	  Equivalently, \(\dim_\kappa = \operatorname{finrank}_\kappa\) is additive on a
319	  short exact sequence \(0 \to W \to V \to V/W \to 0\) of finite-dimensional
320	  \(\kappa\)-vector spaces. This is the additivity that converts the degreewise
321	  short exact sequences of graded components into additive relations among the
322	  coefficients of the Hilbert series.
323	\end{lemma}
324	
325	\begin{lemma}[The rational power series \((1 - X)^{-d}\)]
326	  \label{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
327	  \lean{PowerSeries.invOneSubPow}
328	  \mathlibok
329	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
330	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown}).}
331	  Let \(S\) be a commutative ring and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then \((1 - X)^d\) is a
332	  unit in the power series ring \(S\llbracket X\rrbracket\), and Mathlib names its
333	  inverse
334	  \[
335	    \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(S, d) \;=\; (1 - X)^{-d}
336	      \;\in\; S\llbracket X\rrbracket^{\times}.
337	  \]
338	  Together with the coercion
339	  \(\operatorname{toPowerSeries} : F[X] \to F\llbracket X\rrbracket\) of a
340	  polynomial to its power series, this is the data in which a rational Hilbert
341	  series \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\) is expressed; it is the same rational-series shape
342	  whose eventual coefficients are extracted by
343	  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}.
344	\end{lemma}
345	
346	\begin{lemma}\leanok
347	[Graded Hilbert--Serre: rationality of the Hilbert series]
348	  \label{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}
349	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational}
350	  \uses{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib,
351	    def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero, lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq,
352	    lem:ratHilb_bump, def:graded_subquotientHilb,
353	    lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}
354	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", \S "Noetherian graded
355	  % rings", Proposition \texttt{proposition-graded-hilbert-polynomial} (Tag
356	  % 00K1) (read from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13893-L13905).
357	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
358	  % "Suppose that $S$ is a Noetherian graded ring
359	  %  and $M$ a finite graded $S$-module. Consider the
360	  %  function
361	  %  $$
362	  %  \mathbf{Z} \longrightarrow K'_0(S_0), \quad
363	  %  n \longmapsto [M_n]
364	  %  $$
365	  %  see Lemma \ref{lemma-graded-module-fg}.
366	  %  If $S_{+}$ is generated by elements of degree $1$,
367	  %  then this function is a numerical polynomial."
368	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", Tag 00K1
369	  (proposition on graded Hilbert polynomials); cf.\ [Nitsure], \S 1 (Snapper's
370	  Lemma), [Hartshorne], I.7.}
371	  Let \(R\) be a Noetherian graded ring whose degree-zero part is a field
372	  \(\kappa\) and which is generated as a \(\kappa\)-algebra by finitely many
373	  elements, and let \(M\) be a finitely generated graded \(R\)-module with
374	  finite-dimensional graded components \(M_n\). Then there exist
375	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N \in \mathbb{N}\)
376	  such that for all \(n > N\)
377	  \[
378	    \dim_{\kappa} M_n \;=\; \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\bigr);
379	  \]
380	  that is, the Hilbert series \(\sum_{n \ge 0} (\dim_\kappa M_n)\, X^n\) is, up to
381	  a polynomial correction in low degrees, the rational function
382	  \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\), expressed in the power-series form
383	  \(\operatorname{toPowerSeries}(p) \cdot \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, d)\)
384	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}).
385	
386	  \emph{Status.} This is the substantive half of the graded Hilbert--Serre
387	  theorem (rationality of the Hilbert series of a finitely generated graded
388	  module). It is not covered by Mathlib: \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
389	  supplies only the converse polynomial-extraction step from a rational series, not
390	  the production of the rational series from the module. The argument is realised by
391	  an \emph{ambient subquotient induction} (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}):
392	  the whole induction ranges over pairs of homogeneous submodules \(N' \le N\) of the
393	  single fixed graded module \(M\), and the Hilbert function is the ambient difference
394	  \(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa(N \cap M_n) - \dim_\kappa(N' \cap M_n)\). The class of such
395	  pairs is closed under the kernel and cokernel of multiplication by a degree-one
396	  element (each presented again as an ambient subquotient pair), so the inductive step
397	  never forms a graded quotient ring \(R/(x)\), a graded quotient module \(M/xM\), or a
398	  regrading of a derived carrier. The genuine Mathlib gap is thereby \emph{avoided}
399	  rather than filled; the residual obligation is the short ambient bookkeeping of the
400	  subquotient blocks (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb},
401	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker},
402	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff},
403	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer},
404	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}), each of which manipulates only ambient
405	  families \(n \mapsto N_{\mathrm{aux}} \cap M_n\) of submodules of \(M\).
406	  % NOTE: The power-series rationality toolkit (\cref{subsec:isRatHilb}) is formalized
407	  %   and axiom-clean. As of iter-020 the present theorem IS a closed, axiom-clean Lean
408	  %   declaration (gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational); the ambient subquotient route
409	  %   (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}) sidesteps missing graded-quotient API by
410	  %   working with ambient families of submodules of \(M\).
411	\end{lemma}
412	
413	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1, proof, inductive step (read
414	% from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13938-L13947).
415	% "Let $\overline{M} = M/xM$. Note that the map $x : M \to M$
416	%  is {\it not} a map of graded $S$-modules, since it does
417	%  not map $M_d$ into $M_d$. Namely, for each $d$ we have the
418	%  following short exact sequence
419	%  $$
420	%  0 \to M_d \xrightarrow{x} M_{d + 1} \to \overline{M}_{d + 1} \to 0
421	%  $$
422	%  This proves that $[M_{d + 1}] - [M_d] = [\overline{M}_{d + 1}]$.
423	%  Hence we win by Lemma \ref{lemma-numerical-polynomial}."
424	\begin{proof}
425	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero, lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq,
426	    lem:ratHilb_bump, lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff,
427	    def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
428	    lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff,
429	    lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer, lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb,
430	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
431	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq, lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib,
432	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib,
433	    lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}
434	    \leanok
435	  We argue with the abstract graded \(\kappa\)-algebra
436	  \(R = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} R_n\), whose degree-zero part \(R_0 = \kappa\) is a
437	  field, and the finitely generated graded \(R\)-module
438	  \(M = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} M_n\) with finite-dimensional components \(M_n\);
439	  there is no appeal to any geometric realisation of \(R\) or \(M\).
440	
441	  \emph{Reduction to generation in degree \(1\).} Since \(R\) is Noetherian and
442	  finitely generated as a \(\kappa\)-algebra, it is generated by finitely many
443	  homogeneous elements of positive degree. Replacing \(R\) by a Veronese
444	  subalgebra \(R^{(e)} = \bigoplus_n R_{en}\) for a suitable \(e\) and re-grading
445	  (and \(M\) by the corresponding Veronese module), we may assume \(R\) is
446	  generated by its degree-one part, \(\kappa[R_1] = R\). The degree-one part
447	  \(R_1\) is then a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space whose \(\kappa\)-basis
448	  \(x_1, \dots, x_r\) generates \(R\) as a \(\kappa\)-algebra; \(r = \dim_\kappa R_1\)
449	  is the number on which we induct.
450	  % NOTE: The existence of finitely many degree-one generators (the number r the
451	  % induction is on) is expected to follow from the standing hypotheses --
452	  % "finitely generated κ-algebra" together with "generated in degree 1" -- so no
453	  % new hypothesis on gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational is anticipated. To be
454	  % confirmed when the prover formalizes the assembly: degree-1 generation gives
455	  % R₁ as a finite-dimensional κ-vector space spanning R as a κ-algebra, and a
456	  % κ-basis of R₁ is a finite degree-one generating set.
457	
458	  \emph{Set-up of the ambient data.} Regard \(M\) as the fixed ambient graded
459	  \(\kappa\)-module with internal decomposition \(n \mapsto \mathcal{M}_n := M_n\)
460	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}); the components are finite-dimensional. For each
461	  basis vector \(x_i \in R_1\) let \(t_i : M \to M\) be multiplication by \(x_i\), a
462	  \(\kappa\)-linear endomorphism that raises degree by one (it sends \(M_n\) into
463	  \(M_{n+1}\), \cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}). Since \(R\) is
464	  commutative the \(t_i\) pairwise commute, and they trivially preserve the pair of
465	  homogeneous submodules \((N, N') = (\top, \bot)\). Because \(R\) is generated by
466	  \(x_1, \dots, x_r\) and \(M\) is finitely generated over \(R\), \(M\) is finitely
467	  generated as a module over the polynomial ring \(\kappa[x_1, \dots, x_r]\) acting
468	  through the \(t_i\); this is the finiteness condition required below.
469	
470	  \emph{Invoking the ambient subquotient induction.} Apply
471	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb} to the pair \((N, N') = (\top, \bot)\)
472	  with the \(r\) commuting degree-\((+1)\) endomorphisms \(t_1, \dots, t_r\). It
473	  yields \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\) of order \(r\) for the ambient subquotient Hilbert
474	  function of this pair (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}),
475	  \[
476	    h(n) \;=\; \dim_\kappa(\top \cap \mathcal{M}_n) - \dim_\kappa(\bot \cap \mathcal{M}_n)
477	      \;=\; \dim_\kappa M_n - 0 \;=\; \dim_\kappa M_n .
478	  \]
479	  Hence \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa M_n,\, r)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}):
480	  there are \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\), \(d := r \in \mathbb{N}\) and \(N \in \mathbb{N}\)
481	  with
482	  \[
483	    \dim_\kappa M_n \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\bigr)
484	    \qquad (n > N),
485	  \]
486	  which is the claim, with pole order \(d = r\). The internal mechanics of
487	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb} -- the base case via
488	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero}, and the inductive step assembling the
489	  kernel/cokernel closure (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}), the degreewise
490	  difference identity (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}, built on
491	  \(D_5\), \cref{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}) and the finiteness transfer
492	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}) into
493	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq} and \cref{lem:ratHilb_bump} -- are the Stacks~00K1
494	  induction realised entirely with ambient homogeneous submodules of \(M\).
495	\end{proof}
496	
497	\subsection{The rationality toolkit \texorpdfstring{\(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\)}{IsRatHilb}}
498	\label{subsec:isRatHilb}
499	% NOTE: Declarations in this subsection are currently \texttt{private} in the Lean
500	% source; \lean{} pins may not resolve until they are moved to a dedicated module.
501	
502	The proof of \cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational} runs the Stacks~00K1 induction
503	on the number of degree-one generators. Each inductive step manipulates the
504	\emph{shape} of the Hilbert series -- a numerator polynomial over a power of
505	\((1-X)\) -- rather than the module itself. We isolate that bookkeeping in a
506	single predicate \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\) on functions \(f : \mathbb{N} \to
507	\mathbb{Q}\), together with the closure lemmas the induction consumes. These are
508	project-internal helpers realising standard steps of the Stacks~00K1 argument at
509	the level of power series; Mathlib supplies only the converse extraction
510	(\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}).
511	
512	\begin{lemma}\leanok
513	[Coefficients of \((1-X)^{-1}\) times a series are partial sums]
514	  \label{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul}
515	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul}
516	  \uses{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
517	  Let \(F \in \mathbb{Q}\llbracket X\rrbracket\) be a power series. Then for every
518	  \(n \in \mathbb{N}\) the \(n\)-th coefficient of
519	  \(\operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, 1)\cdot F = (1-X)^{-1}\cdot F\)
520	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}) is the partial sum of the coefficients of
521	  \(F\):
522	  \[
523	    \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl((1-X)^{-1}\cdot F\bigr)
524	    \;=\; \sum_{k=0}^{n} \bigl[X^k\bigr] F .
525	  \]
526	  Equivalently, multiplication by \((1-X)^{-1} = \sum_{m\ge 0} X^m\) is the
527	  summation operator on coefficient sequences. Proved directly in Lean from the
528	  identity \(\operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q},1) = \sum_{m\ge 0} X^m\) and
529	  the Cauchy product.
530	\end{lemma}
531	
532	\begin{lemma}\leanok
533	[Antidifference of a rational Hilbert series]
534	  \label{lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}
535	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rationalHilbert_antidiff}
536	  \uses{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
537	  Let \(H, \delta : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\), let \(q \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and
538	  \(e, N \in \mathbb{N}\). Suppose that for all \(n > N\) the sequence \(\delta\)
539	  is the coefficient sequence of the rational series \(q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\),
540	  \[
541	    \delta(n) \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\bigr),
542	  \]
543	  and that \(\delta\) is the first difference of \(H\) beyond \(N\), i.e.
544	  \(H(n+1) - H(n) = \delta(n+1)\) for all \(n > N\). Then there is a numerator
545	  polynomial \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) such that for all \(n > N\)
546	  \[
547	    H(n) \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\bigr).
548	  \]
549	  That is, summing a coefficient sequence (anti-differencing) raises the pole
550	  order by one: the partial-sum series of \(q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\) equals
551	  \(p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\) up to a polynomial correction in low degrees. This is
552	  the power-series heart of the Stacks~00K1 inductive step.
553	\end{lemma}
554	
555	\begin{proof}
556	  \uses{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
557	  \leanok
558	  Write \(F = q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\). Multiplying by
559	  \((1-X)^{-1}\) and using the factorisation
560	  \((1-X)^{-(e+1)} = (1-X)^{-1}\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\)
561	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}), \cref{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul} gives
562	  \(\bigl[X^m\bigr]\bigl(q\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\bigr) = \sum_{k=0}^{m}[X^k]F\), the
563	  partial sums of \(F\). On the other hand, telescoping the first-difference
564	  hypothesis from \(N+1\) expresses \(H(N+1+j)\) as \(H(N+1)\) plus a window sum
565	  of the coefficients of \(F\). Splitting the partial sum
566	  \(\sum_{k=0}^{n}[X^k]F\) at \(N+2\) and absorbing the resulting constant
567	  discrepancy into a numerator \(C\,(1-X)^{e}\) (a constant function is the
568	  order-\((e+1)\) coefficient sequence of \(C\,(1-X)^{e}\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)} =
569	  C\,(1-X)^{-1}\)), the explicit numerator
570	  \(p = C\,(1-X)^{e} + q\) realises \(H(n)\) as
571	  \([X^n]\bigl(p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\bigr)\) for all \(n > N\).
572	\end{proof}
573	
574	\begin{definition}\leanok
575	[Rational Hilbert function]
576	  \label{def:ratHilb}
577	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb}
578	  \uses{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
579	  \textit{Source: Stacks 00K1.}
580	  For a function \(f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), say
581	  that \(f\) is a \emph{rational Hilbert function of order \(d\)}, written
582	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\), when there exist a numerator polynomial
583	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and an \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) such that for all \(n > N\)
584	  \[
585	    f(n) \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p\cdot(1-X)^{-d}\bigr)
586	    \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(\operatorname{toPowerSeries}(p)\cdot
587	      \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, d)\bigr)
588	  \]
589	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}). This is the ``eventually equal to the
590	  coefficient sequence of a rational series \(p\cdot(1-X)^{-d}\)'' predicate that
591	  the graded Hilbert--Serre induction tracks; its conclusion shape is exactly the
592	  one consumed by \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}.
593	\end{definition}
594	
595	\begin{lemma}\leanok
596	[Base case: eventually-zero functions are rational of order \(0\)]
597	  \label{lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero}
598	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.ofEventuallyZero}
599	  \uses{def:ratHilb}
600	  If \(f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) is eventually zero -- there is
601	  \(N\) with \(f(n) = 0\) for all \(n > N\) -- then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, 0)\)
602	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}). Indeed \(f\) is then the coefficient sequence of the
603	  numerator polynomial \(0\) over \((1-X)^{0} = 1\). This is the base case of the
604	  Stacks~00K1 induction (no degree-one generators). Proved directly in Lean.
605	\end{lemma}
606	
607	\begin{lemma}\leanok
608	[Raising the pole order]
609	  \label{lem:ratHilb_bump}
610	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.bump}
611	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
612	  If \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}) then
613	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d+1)\): multiplying the numerator by \((1-X)\) cancels
614	  one factor of \((1-X)^{-1}\), so the same coefficient sequence is realised at
615	  pole order \(d+1\). This lets two rational Hilbert functions of different
616	  orders be brought to a common denominator. Proved directly in Lean using
617	  \((1-X)\cdot(1-X)^{-(d+1)} = (1-X)^{-d}\) (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}).
618	\end{lemma}
619	
620	\begin{lemma}\leanok
621	[Closure under difference]
622	  \label{lem:ratHilb_sub}
623	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.sub}
624	  \uses{def:ratHilb}
625	  If \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\) and \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(g, d)\)
626	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}) at the same order \(d\), then
627	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto f(n) - g(n),\, d)\): the numerator of the
628	  difference is the difference of the numerators (over the common denominator
629	  \((1-X)^{-d}\)). Proved directly in Lean.
630	\end{lemma}
631	
632	\begin{lemma}\leanok
633	[Closure under right shift]
634	  \label{lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}
635	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.shiftRight}
636	  \uses{def:ratHilb}
637	  If \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}) then
638	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto f(n-1),\, d)\): the right shift on coefficient
639	  sequences is multiplication of the series by \(X\), so the numerator
640	  \(p\) is replaced by \(X\cdot p\) at the same pole order. Proved directly in
641	  Lean.
642	\end{lemma}
643	
644	\begin{lemma}\leanok
645	[Antidifference step for the predicate]
646	  \label{lem:ratHilb_antidiff}
647	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.antidiff}
648	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}
649	  Let \(H, g : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) and \(e, N \in \mathbb{N}\). If
650	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(g, e)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}) and \(g\) is the first
651	  difference of \(H\) beyond \(N\) (\(H(n+1) - H(n) = g(n+1)\) for all
652	  \(n > N\)), then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(H, e+1)\). This is the predicate-level
653	  packaging of \cref{lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}: a finite difference raises
654	  the pole order by one, the inductive heart of graded Hilbert--Serre.
655	\end{lemma}
656	
657	\begin{proof}
658	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}
659	  \leanok
660	  Unfolding \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(g, e)\) supplies a numerator \(q\) and a
661	  threshold \(N_g\) with \(g(n) = [X^n](q\cdot(1-X)^{-e})\) for \(n > N_g\).
662	  Applying \cref{lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff} with this \(q\) and threshold
663	  \(\max(N, N_g)\) -- whose two hypotheses are exactly the rational form of \(g\)
664	  and the first-difference identity for \(H\) -- yields a numerator \(p\) with
665	  \(H(n) = [X^n](p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)})\) for \(n > \max(N, N_g)\), i.e.
666	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(H, e+1)\).
667	\end{proof}
668	
669	\begin{lemma}\leanok
670	[Inductive-step engine from a degreewise short exact sequence]
671	  \label{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq}
672	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq}
673	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_antidiff, lem:ratHilb_sub, lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}
674	  Let \(h_M, h_C, h_K : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) and \(d, N \in \mathbb{N}\).
675	  Suppose \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_C, d)\) and \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_K, d)\)
676	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}), and that for all \(n > N\)
677	  \[
678	    h_M(n+1) - h_M(n) \;=\; h_C(n+1) - h_K(n).
679	  \]
680	  Then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_M, d+1)\). This packages the entire power-series
681	  side of the Stacks~00K1 inductive step: \(h_M\) is the Hilbert function of
682	  \(M\), and \(h_C, h_K\) are the Hilbert functions of the cokernel
683	  \(C = M/xM\) and kernel \(K = \ker(x : M \to M(1))\) of multiplication by a
684	  degree-one element \(x\); the displayed identity is the alternating-sum
685	  consequence of the degreewise short exact sequence
686	  \(0 \to K_n \to M_n \xrightarrow{x} M_{n+1} \to C_{n+1} \to 0\). Given the
687	  inductive hypothesis that \(h_C, h_K\) are rational of order \(d\), the
688	  conclusion is rationality of \(h_M\) at order \(d+1\).
689	\end{lemma}
690	
691	\begin{proof}
692	  \uses{lem:ratHilb_antidiff, lem:ratHilb_sub, lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}
693	  \leanok
694	  Set \(g(n) = h_C(n) - h_K(n-1)\). By closure under right shift
695	  (\cref{lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}) \(n \mapsto h_K(n-1)\) is rational of order
696	  \(d\), and by closure under difference (\cref{lem:ratHilb_sub}) so is \(g\). The
697	  hypothesis rewrites as \(h_M(n+1) - h_M(n) = g(n+1)\) for \(n > N\) (since
698	  \(g(n+1) = h_C(n+1) - h_K(n)\)). Applying the antidifference step
699	  (\cref{lem:ratHilb_antidiff}) to \(H = h_M\) and this \(g\) gives
700	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_M, d+1)\).
701	\end{proof}
702	
703	\begin{theorem}[Hilbert polynomial from the section module]
704	  \label{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}
705	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomialOfSectionModule}
706	  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
707	    lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
708	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
709	  % Polynomials" (read from
710	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L478).
711	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
712	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
713	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
714	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
715	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$. Then for each $s\in S$,
716	  %  we get a polynomial ${\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]$
717	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
718	  %  the restriction $\F_s = F|_{X_s}$ of $\F$ to the fiber $X_s$ over $s$,
719	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle $L_s = L|_{X_s}$."
720	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
721	  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
722	  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
723	  Then there is a unique polynomial \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
724	  such that for all \(m \gg 0\)
725	  \[
726	    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
727	    \;=\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr).
728	  \]
729	  This \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is the Hilbert polynomial of
730	  \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}.
731	\end{theorem}
732	
733	\begin{proof}
734	  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
735	    lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
736	  By \cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg} the section graded module
737	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded module over the
738	  Noetherian graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\), with finite-dimensional components and
739	  degree-zero part the field \(\kappa(s)\). Applying the abstract graded
740	  Hilbert--Serre rationality lemma (\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}) to the
741	  graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra \(R = R(X_s, L_s)\) and the finitely generated
742	  graded module \(M = M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) exhibits the graded Hilbert
743	  function
744	  \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
745	  as the eventual coefficient function of a rational series: there are
746	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N\) with
747	  \(\dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
748	  = [X^m]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\) for \(m > N\). Apply
749	  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib} over the characteristic-zero field
750	  \(F = \mathbb{Q}\) to this pair \((p, d)\): it yields a unique polynomial
751	  \(h \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) whose values \(h(n)\) eventually equal
752	  \([X^n]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\). Setting
753	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} := h\) (with the variable renamed \(\lambda\)) gives a
754	  polynomial with \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m) = \dim_{\kappa(s)}
755	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) for \(m \gg 0\). Uniqueness
756	  is the \(\exists!\) clause of \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}: any two
757	  polynomials agreeing with the Hilbert function for \(m \gg 0\) agree at
758	  infinitely many points, hence are equal.
759	\end{proof}

40	### Decision: Route A — assemble a global `Presentation M` from `QuasicoherentData`
41	
42	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE in the local→global direction. `SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData`
43	  (Quasicoherent.lean:201) holds a cover `X : I → C` + per-member `(M.over (X i)).Presentation`.
44	  Its API only goes the *other* way or shuffles local data: `Presentation.quasicoherentData`
45	  (Pres → QCData), `QuasicoherentData.bind`/`shrink`/`localGeneratorsData`,
46	  `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop`. The global `GeneratingSections` structure
47	  (Generators.lean:45) has **no constructor from `LocalGeneratorsData`**. There is no
48	  "globally generated on affine/qcqs", no "sections generate a QC sheaf on affine".
49	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong-to-attempt. Route A is strictly HARDER than the stalk/section
50	  route: a global `Presentation` additionally needs *global generation by sections*
51	  (QC on affine ⟹ generated by global sections), which itself needs the same
52	  section-localization/affine-vanishing input plus a kernel-is-also-QC step.
53	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (do **not** target Route A).

91	## The irreducible content (the actual gap)
92	
93	All three routes funnel through ONE Mathlib-absent fact (Stacks tag 01HA / Hartshorne II.5.2):
94	
95	> **For a quasi-coherent `M` on `Spec R` and `f : R`, the restriction `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))`
96	> is `IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)`** (equivalently `M.stalk p` is the localization
97	> of `Γ(M,⊤)` at `p.primeCompl`).
98	
99	Proof skeleton from `QuasicoherentData` (genuine multi-step descent):
100	1. **Refine the QC cover to basic opens.** `PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`; restrict each
101	   `(M.over (X i)).Presentation` to a basic open `D(g_a) ⊆ X_i` via `Presentation.map` along the
102	   `pushforward`/`over` restriction functor (Quasicoherent.lean:179). `CompactSpace (PrimeSpectrum R)`
103	   (Spectrum/Prime/Topology.lean:291) ⟹ a **finite** subcover; the `g_a` generate the unit ideal.
104	2. **Locally `M` is a tilde sheaf.** On `D(g_a) = Spec R_{g_a}`, the local presentation +
105	   `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` ⟹ `M.over (D g_a) ≅ tilde(N_a)`, `N_a = Γ(M, D(g_a))`,
106	   over the ring `Γ(Spec R, D(g_a)) = R_{g_a}` (`AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalization_basicOpen`,
107	   AffineScheme.lean:650).
108	3. **Sheaf equalizer + flat descent.** Sheaf condition over the finite cover {D(g_a)} gives
109	   `0 → Γ(M,⊤) → ∏ N_a → ∏ Γ(M, D(g_a g_b))`; localizing at `powers f` is exact
110	   (`IsLocalization.flat`, Flat/Localization.lean:36; localization commutes with finite limits)
111	   and matches the same equalizer for `Γ(M,D(f))`, yielding `Γ(M,⊤)_f ≅ Γ(M,D(f))`.
112	4. Stalk version: `M.stalk p = colim_{D(g)∋p} Γ(M,D(g)) = colim N_g = N_p`.
113	
114	## Recommendation
115	
116	gap1 is **genuine multi-step descent with no current Mathlib idiom**. Do NOT attempt a single
117	lemma or Route A (global `Presentation`). Target **Route C** and split the work as:
118	
119	- **G1-core** (the gap): `QC M ⟹ IsLocalizedModule (powers f) (Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f)))`. This is the
120	  keystone; build it from `QuasicoherentData` via the cover-refine → local-tilde → flat-equalizer
121	  skeleton above. Reuses `isLocalization_basicOpen`, `IsLocalization.flat`, `CompactSpace`.
122	- **G1-assemble** (cheap, all Mathlib glue): from G1-core, prove each basic-open component of
123	  `modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ` iso via `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv` +
124	  `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` + `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app`, then
125	  `isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` + `FullyFaithful.reflectsIsomorphisms` to land `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`.

129	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ` derives section-localization *from* `[IsIso
130	fromTildeΓ]` — backwards for breaking circularity. The honest dependency is:
131	**G1-core (section-localization from QC data) → gap1 (IsIso fromTildeΓ) → general-scheme

9	# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer
10	
11	This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
12	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
13	per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
15	*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
16	Grassmannian) together with its representability.
17	
18	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
19	
20	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
21	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
22	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
23	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
24	
25	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
26	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
27	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
28	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
29	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
30	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
31	
32	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def) — the
33	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
34	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
35	
36	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem)
37	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
38	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
39	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
40	
41	## Note on type expressivity
42	
43	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
44	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
45	
46	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
47	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
48	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
49	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
50	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
51	- `Grassmannian.representable` packages the
52	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
53	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
54	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
55	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
56	  that isomorphism family).
57	
58	## Mathlib status
59	
60	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
61	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
62	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
63	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
64	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
65	- `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
66	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
67	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
68	
69	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
70	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
71	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
72	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
73	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
74

92	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
93	
94	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
95	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
96	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
97	polynomial is the function
98	
99	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
100	
101	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
102	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
105	III.5.2). -/
106	
107	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
108	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
109	
110	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
111	
112	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
113	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
114	
115	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
116	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
117	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
118	
119	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
120	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
121	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
122	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
123	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
124	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
125	    Polynomial ℚ :=
126	  sorry
127	
128	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
129	
130	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
131	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
132	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
133	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
134	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
135	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
136	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
137	
138	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
139	
140	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
141	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
142	
143	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
144	
145	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
146	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
147	
148	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
149	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
150	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
151	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
152	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
153	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
154	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
155	
156	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
157	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
158	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
159	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
160	is a typed `sorry`. -/
161	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
162	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
163	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
164	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
165	  sorry
166	
167	end Scheme
168	
169	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
170	
171	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
172	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
173	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
174	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
175	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
176	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
177	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
178	
179	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
180	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
181	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
182	
183	namespace Scheme
184	
185	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
186	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
187	
188	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
189	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
190	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
191	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
192	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
193	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
194	
195	iter-177+: the body re-exports `QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) (?) V Φ_d`, where
196	`Φ_d : Polynomial ℚ` is the constant polynomial `d`. For the iter-176
197	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
198	noncomputable def Grassmannian {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
199	    (_V : S.Modules) (_d : ℕ) :
200	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
201	  sorry
202	
203	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
204	
205	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,
206	and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d)` of
207	`Grassmannian` is representable by a smooth projective `S`-scheme
208	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a
209	tautological rank-`d` quotient `π* V ↠ U`. The determinant line bundle
210	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
211	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
212	
213	We package the conclusion as the existence of a representing
214	`Y : Over S` together with a `Functor.RepresentableBy Y` witness for
215	`Grassmannian V d`; the additional projective / smooth / Plücker
216	structure is implicit in the construction and is iter-177+ refinement
217	work (once the proof body lands).
218	
219	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian":
220	glue the `binom(r, d)` affine charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the
221	Plücker cocycle, verify separatedness via the diagonal cut, verify
222	properness by the DVR valuative criterion, build the tautological
223	quotient `U`, exhibit the Plücker embedding via the determinant line
224	bundle. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
225	theorem Grassmannian.representable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
226	    (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
227	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
228	  sorry
229	
230	end Scheme

232	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Quot/Grassmannian predicates
233	
234	These declarations build the support/freeness predicates of
235	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`, §"Support and freeness
236	predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no rank-`d` local
237	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme, so it is built here. -/
238	
239	namespace SheafOfModules
240	
241	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
242	
243	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
244	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
245	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
246	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
247	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
248	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).
249	
250	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
251	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
252	`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
253	def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
254	  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
255	    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
256	      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))
257	
258	end SheafOfModules

260	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator ideal sheaf and schematic support
261	
262	These declarations build the annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules and the
263	support/properness predicates of `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`,
264	§"Support and freeness predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no
265	annihilator ideal sheaf for sheaves of modules on a scheme, nor a schematic-support
266	or proper-support predicate, so they are built here.
267	
268	The annihilator is packaged via `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, exactly mirroring
269	Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker` (which is `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`):
270	`ofIdeals` produces *the largest ideal sheaf contained in* an arbitrary affine-local
271	family of ideals, so the structure's `map_ideal_basicOpen` coherence is discharged
272	internally and need not be supplied at definition time. The basic-open coherence that
273	makes the local annihilators agree with `ofIdeals` (the analogue of `Hom.ker_apply`,
274	`def:modules_annihilator`) is the separate characterization lemma `annihilator_ideal`,
275	which depends on the not-yet-closed QCoh→localization bridge
276	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`) together with
277	the algebra engine `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`
278	(`lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`); see the handoff in
279	`task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/
280	
281	namespace Scheme.Modules
282	
283	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
284	
285	/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
286	(`def:modules_annihilator`).
287	
288	On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
289	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
290	is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
291	in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
292	sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
293	the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
294	characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).
295	
296	This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
297	for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
298	noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
299	  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)
300	
301	/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
302	module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
303	of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
304	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
305	lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
306	    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
307	  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _
308	
309	/-- The **schematic support** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
310	(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator
311	ideal sheaf `annihilator F`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
312	noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
313	  (annihilator F).subscheme
314	
315	/-- The canonical closed immersion of the schematic support into the ambient scheme,
316	realizing `schematicSupport F` as a closed subscheme of `X` (`def:schematic_support`).
317	This is the `IdealSheafData.subschemeι` of the annihilator ideal sheaf; it is a
318	`IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` immersion onto the support. Project-local because
319	`annihilator` is. -/
320	noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=
321	  (annihilator F).subschemeι
322	
323	/-- The sheaf of modules `F` **has proper support over `S` along `f`**
324	(`def:has_proper_support`): the composite of the schematic-support immersion with
325	`f : X ⟶ S` is a proper morphism. Since `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper` is stable under
326	base change, this condition is preserved by pullback, as required by the Quot functor's
327	pullback action. Project-local because `schematicSupport` is. -/
328	def HasProperSupport {S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
329	  IsProper (schematicSupportι F ≫ f)
330	
331	end Scheme.Modules
332	
333	end AlgebraicGeometry
334	
335	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization
336	
337	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
338	built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
339	`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`
340	requires the algebraic fact that, for a *finitely generated* module, the
341	annihilator commutes with localization:
342	`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this
343	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.
344	-/
345	
346	namespace Module
347	
348	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
349	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
350	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
351	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
352	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
353	
354	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
355	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
356	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
357	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
358	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
359	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
360	
361	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
362	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
363	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
364	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
365	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
366	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
367	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
368	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
369	  classical
370	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
371	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
372	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
373	    intro r h
374	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
375	    intro x
376	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
377	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
378	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
379	    | zero => simp
380	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]
381	    | smul c a _ ha => rw [smul_comm, ha, smul_zero]
382	  apply le_antisymm
383	  · -- `Ann Rₚ Mₚ ⊆ (Ann R M).map`: clear one common denominator over the generators
384	    intro y hy
385	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at hy
386	    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S y
387	    dsimp only at hy ⊢
388	    have hgen : ∀ m ∈ t, ∃ u : S, (u : R) • a • m = 0 := by
389	      intro m hm
390	      have hz := hy (IsLocalizedModule.mk' f m (1 : S))
391	      rw [IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_eq_zero,
392	        IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S f] at hz
393	      obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hz
394	      exact ⟨u, hu⟩
395	    choose u hu using hgen
396	    obtain ⟨U, hU⟩ : ∃ U : S, ∀ m ∈ t, (U : R) • a • m = 0 := by
397	      refine ⟨∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2, ?_⟩
398	      intro m hm
399	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
400	        Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem (fun x : t => u x.1 x.2) (Finset.mem_attach t ⟨m, hm⟩)
401	      have hcoe : ((∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2 : S) : R) = (u m hm : R) * (c : R) := by
402	        rw [hc]; push_cast; ring
403	      rw [hcoe, mul_smul, smul_comm, hu m hm, smul_zero]
404	    have hUa : (U : R) * a ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
405	      apply key; intro m hm; rw [mul_smul]; exact hU m hm
406	    have heq : IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ a s
407	        = (algebraMap R Rₚ ((U : R) * a)) * IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ 1 (U * s) := by
408	      rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_eq_mul_mk'_one, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq]
409	      push_cast; ring
410	    rw [heq]
411	    exact Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hUa)
412	  · -- `(Ann R M).map ⊆ Ann Rₚ Mₚ`: the image of an annihilator annihilates
413	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
414	    intro a ha
415	    rw [Ideal.mem_comap, Module.mem_annihilator]
416	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at ha
417	    intro x
418	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_surjective S f x
419	    dsimp only [Function.uncurry]

425	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections localize on a basic open
426	
427	This section builds, bottom-up, toward the keystone
428	`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`: for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M`
429	on a scheme `X`, an affine open `U`, and `f ∈ Γ(X,U)`, the restriction
430	`M(U) → M(D(f))` exhibits `M(D(f))` as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X,U)`.
431	
432	The substance is the affine (Spec-local) computation: over `Spec R`, a quasi-coherent
433	sheaf is `Ñ = tilde N` for `N = Γ(M)`, and the basic-open restriction of `Ñ` is the
434	module localization map. Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde` namespace already carries
435	the localization fact for `tilde N` *as the map out of `N`* (the instance
436	`IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (tilde.toOpen N (basicOpen f)).hom`). The first building
437	block below repackages this as a statement about the *presheaf restriction map* of `Ñ`
438	itself (from global sections to `D(f)`), which is the form the downstream scheme-level
439	argument consumes after the affine identification `M|_U ≅ Ñ`.
440	
441	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does **not** prove that an arbitrary quasi-coherent sheaf
442	on `Spec R` lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the comment in
443	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean` says this "will later be shown"); the
444	equivalence `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` is a genuine gap. Consequently the keystone for an
445	*arbitrary* quasi-coherent `M` is gated on that bridge (`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso
446	M.fromTildeΓ`); the building blocks here are stated for `tilde N` directly, and for a
447	general `M : (Spec R).Modules` under the explicit hypothesis `[IsIso M.fromTildeΓ]`
448	(equivalently, `M` in the essential image of `tilde`). -/
449	
450	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
451	
452	open CategoryTheory Limits
453	
454	/-- **Basic-open restriction of a `tilde` sheaf is a module localization.**
455	
456	For `N : ModuleCat R` and `f : R`, the presheaf restriction map of the associated sheaf
457	`Ñ = tilde N` from global sections `Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(Ñ, D(f))` exhibits the
458	latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
459	
460	This is the affine, Spec-local heart of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. Mathlib
461	carries the localization fact for the map `tilde.toOpen N (D f) : N → Γ(Ñ, D(f))` out of
462	`N`; since `tilde.toOpen N ⊤ : N → Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is an isomorphism and
463	`tilde.toOpen N (D f) = tilde.toOpen N ⊤ ≫ restriction` (`tilde.toOpen_res`), precomposing
464	the localization map with the inverse isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`)
465	transfers the property to the restriction map. Project-local: Mathlib states the fact only
466	for the map out of `N`, not for the presheaf restriction of `Ñ`. -/
467	theorem isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : ModuleCat.{u} R) (f : R) :
468	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
469	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
470	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
471	  set res := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
472	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op with hresdef
473	  have hres := tilde.toOpen_res N ⊤ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
474	    (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤))
475	  -- `e : N ≃ₗ Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is the global-sections isomorphism of the tilde sheaf.
476	  set e : N ≃ₗ[R] _ := (tilde.isoTop N).toLinearEquiv with hedef
477	  have key : (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom = res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap := by
478	    rw [hedef, ← hres]; rfl
479	  have hinst0 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
480	      (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom := inferInstance
481	  rw [key] at hinst0
482	  set g := res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap with hg
483	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) g := hinst0
484	  have h2 := IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := g) e.symm
485	  have he : g ∘ₗ e.symm.toLinearMap = res.hom := by
486	    apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
487	    change res.hom (e (e.symm x)) = res.hom x
488	    rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
489	  rw [he] at h2
490	  exact h2
491	
492	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a sheaf in the essential image of `tilde`.**
493	
494	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` whose tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an
495	isomorphism (equivalently, `M` lies in the essential image of the `tilde` functor — the
496	honest Spec-affine stand-in for quasi-coherence, see the section header), the presheaf
497	restriction map of `M` from global sections `Γ(M, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(M, D(f))`
498	exhibits the latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
499	
500	This transports `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` across the isomorphism
501	`M.fromTildeΓ : tilde N ⟶ M` (where `N = Γ(M, ⊤)`): the induced presheaf isomorphism is
502	natural in the open, so on each of `⊤` and `D(f)` it provides an `R`-linear isomorphism
503	intertwining the two restriction maps. Post- and pre-composing the localization map for
504	`tilde N` with these isomorphisms (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`,
505	`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`) yields the property for `M`.
506	
507	Project-local: it is the affine engine of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. The
508	general quasi-coherent case additionally requires the (currently Mathlib-absent) bridge
509	`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. -/
510	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
511	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso M.fromTildeΓ] (f : R) :
512	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
513	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
514	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
515	  -- the presheaf-level isomorphism induced by the (iso) counit `M.fromTildeΓ`
516	  let ψ := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget (ModuleCat R) (Spec R)).map (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ)
517	  haveI : IsIso ψ := inferInstance
518	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens))) := inferInstance
519	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))) := inferInstance
520	  -- the component isomorphisms as `R`-linear equivalences
521	  let eTop : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)))).toLinearEquiv
522	  let eDf : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))).toLinearEquiv
523	  -- the restriction map of `tilde N` (localizes by `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict`)
524	  let rt := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj
525	        (tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)))).presheaf.map
526	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
527	  -- naturality square of `ψ` for `D(f) ⟶ ⊤`
528	  have hnat := ψ.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op
529	  have hnathom := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hnat
530	  rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp] at hnathom
531	  haveI hrt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) rt :=
532	    isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) f
533	  haveI step1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) :=
534	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := rt) (e := eDf)
535	  haveI step2 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
536	      ((eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) ∘ₗ eTop.symm.toLinearMap) :=
537	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f)
538	      (f := eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) (e := eTop.symm)
539	  -- identify the target restriction map with `(eDf ∘ rt) ∘ eTop⁻¹`
540	  convert step2 using 1
541	  apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
542	  have hc := LinearMap.congr_fun hnathom (eTop.symm x)
543	  simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply] at hc ⊢
544	  refine (?_ : _ = _).trans hc.symm
545	  congr 1
546	  exact (eTop.apply_symm_apply x).symm
547	
548	end AlgebraicGeometry

2592	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization for a quasi-coherent sheaf on $\Spec R$ (G1-core)]
2593	  \label{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}
2594	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent}
2595	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
2596	  % NOTE: the genuine Mathlib-absent foundational gap; the Lean decl does NOT yet exist. This is the
2597	  % affine instance (X = Spec R, U = ⊤) of \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}, proved
2598	  % DIRECTLY from QuasicoherentData (no IsIso/Presentation assumption), and is what gap1 and the
2599	  % general keystone both reduce to. Build target for the QUOT mathlib-build lane (route in
2600	  % analogies/quot-qcoh-affine-globalization.md, Route C / Stacks 01HA).
2601	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", tag 01HA (\S Quasi-coherent sheaves,
2602	  % "$\mathcal{F}(D(f)) = \mathcal{F}(X)_f$ for $\mathcal{F}$ quasi-coherent on affine $X$")
2603	  % (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex / stacks-properties.tex). Cf. [Hartshorne], II.5.2.
2604	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01HA; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.2.}
2605	  Let \(M\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) and \(f \in R\). Then the section
2606	  restriction map \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\) as the
2607	  localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\); i.e.\ it is
2608	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
2609	\end{lemma}
2610	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: the descent is the standard cover-by-basic-opens + flat-localization argument;
2611	% transcribed in project notation below (Stacks 01HA proof; references/stacks-properties.tex).
2612	\begin{proof}
2613	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
2614	  Write \(N = \Gamma(M, \top)\). The map is built and verified in four steps (the cover-refine →
2615	  local-tilde → flat-equalizer descent):
2616	  \begin{enumerate}
2617	    \item \emph{Refine the quasi-coherence cover to basic opens.} The quasi-coherence datum gives an
2618	      open cover of \(\Spec R\) on each member of which \(M\) is a cokernel of free module sheaves.
2619	      Basic opens \(D(g)\) form a basis of \(\Spec R\), so refine to a cover by basic opens; since
2620	      \(\Spec R\) is quasi-compact, finitely many \(D(g_1), \dots, D(g_n)\) suffice, and the \(g_a\)
2621	      generate the unit ideal.
2622	    \item \emph{Locally \(M\) is a $\widetilde{(-)}$ sheaf.} On each \(D(g_a) \cong \Spec R_{g_a}\)
2623	      the local cokernel-of-frees presentation makes \(M|_{D(g_a)} \cong \widetilde{N_a}\) for the
2624	      \(R_{g_a}\)-module \(N_a = \Gamma(M, D(g_a))\), using that \(\Gamma(\Spec R, D(g_a)) = R_{g_a}\)
2625	      (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}).
2626	    \item \emph{Sheaf equalizer + flat descent.} The sheaf condition over the finite cover
2627	      \(\{D(g_a)\}\) presents \(N = \Gamma(M, \top)\) as the equalizer
2628	      \(N \to \prod_a N_a \rightrightarrows \prod_{a,b} \Gamma(M, D(g_a g_b))\). Localizing this
2629	      finite equalizer at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) is exact (localization is flat, hence preserves
2630	      finite limits), and the localized diagram is the same equalizer computing \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\)
2631	      over the cover \(\{D(g_a) \cap D(f)\}\). Hence \(N_f \cong \Gamma(M, D(f))\), compatibly with
2632	      the restriction map.
2633	    \item Therefore \(\Gamma(M,\top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is the localization \(N \to N_f\), i.e.\
2634	      \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\).
2635	  \end{enumerate}
2636	  The flat-localization-of-a-finite-equalizer step is the substantive content; everything else is
2637	  the affine dictionary already available.
2638	\end{proof}
